solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int minAddToMakeValid(String s) {
        int left = 0, right = 0;
        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            if (c == '(') {
                left++;
            } else {
                if (left > 0) {
                    left--;
                } else {
                    right++;
                }
            }
        }
        return left + right;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a greedy approach to count the number of unmatched opening and closing parentheses. It iterates through the string once, keeping track of unmatched left and right parentheses. The total number of required insertions is the sum of unmatched left and right parentheses. The approach is efficient with constant space and linear time.>
